name: Jordan

profile.shopping_behaviour: Grailed and Depop are primary sources; waits months for right pieces. Scours Brick Lane vintage for Japanese heritage brands and American workwear. Occasionally buys new from Blue in Green, but secondhand-first by principle.
profile.personality: Passionate about fabric weights, construction, and brand provenance—discusses denim oz and Freewheelers' pattern-making extensively. Frustrated by fast fashion's prevalence and heritage piece scarcity in his size. Values longevity and patina over trends.
profile.style_identity: Rotates Iron Heart, Samurai, vintage Sugar Cane, Buzz Rickson's, and The Real McCoy's denim. 1940s military silhouettes, raw denim with natural fading, CPO jackets. Refuses stretch/synthetics; requires natural fibres and selvedge. Admires Kapital and Engineered Garments but rejects retail prices.

preview_description: Ethan curates vintage workwear, prioritizing secondhand Iron Heart jeans, Samurai, and Japanese brands like Warehouse & Co. on r/rawdenim. His £30k salary supports his obsession with grail pieces through secondhand hunting, though he regrets missing Kapital century denim.

preview_attributes.key_brands: Iron Heart, Samurai, The Real McCoy's, Sugar Cane, Buzz Rickson's, Warehouse & Co.
preview_attributes.style_identity: Japanese heritage workwear with vintage military influences
preview_attributes.shopping_triggers: Fabric provenance, construction quality, natural fade potential, secondhand grails at fair prices
preview_attributes.pain_points: Retail pricing on Japanese brands, lack of size availability in archive pieces, peers who don't understand buying £200 used jeans
